summary: FreshView Services is a local exterior-cleaning company serving homeowners in Acworth and Marietta, GA, offering house/siding washing, paver cleaning and restoration, roof washing, and commercial power washing. It runs on a quote-to-booking model: customers request a free no-obligation quote, receive pricing, and book one-off jobs, with repeat revenue coming from informal seasonal re-bookings. Its primary audience is residential homeowners (30-60), with small commercial property owners and landlords as a secondary segment.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Build trust before the crew arrives and set expectations so job day goes smoothly, driving toward the 'aha' walk-around and a 5-star review.
campaigns.trigger: Job booked / date confirmed (fires within seconds of confirmation).
campaigns.segment: New customers with a confirmed first job; branch by service type (house wash, roof, pavers, commercial).
campaigns.rationale: FreshView is greenfield with no post-booking sequence, and its biggest pre-job hesitation is trusting a stranger around the property — a warm, reassuring welcome converts that anxiety into confidence and sets up the dramatic before/after that earns reviews and re-bookings.

emails.purpose: Confirm the booking, set expectations for job day, and pre-empt the trust objection by introducing the crew and the satisfaction guarantee.
emails.subject: You're booked! Here's exactly what happens on job day
emails.preview: Meet your crew, a 2-minute prep checklist, and our satisfaction guarantee.
keyPoints: Confirm the service, date, and rough arrival window so they know what to expect
Introduce the local FreshView crew by name and reassure them their property is in careful, insured hands
Simple prep checklist: move vehicles, unlock the gate, bring in pets, clear the work area
Restate the satisfaction guarantee and a direct phone/text (678-223-0550) to reach a real person before the visit

campaigns.name: Quote-to-Booking Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert free no-obligation quote requests that haven't booked within 48 hours into confirmed jobs.
campaigns.trigger: Quote request submitted; no booking confirmed within 48 hours (plus behavioral nudges in parallel).
campaigns.segment: Quote requesters who haven't booked; branch by quoted service type and by residential vs. commercial.
campaigns.rationale: Quote follow-up today is fully manual with no drip, so warm leads go cold — this is the single biggest revenue leak and directly serves FreshView's stated #1 goal of turning quote requests into booked jobs.

emails.purpose: Re-engage a stalled quote by handling the DIY-vs-pro and price objection with proof, and remove all friction from booking a date.
emails.subject: Still weighing that quote for your siding?
emails.preview: DIY rentals add up fast — here's why your Acworth neighbors let us handle it.
emails.cta: Reply to lock in your date
keyPoints: Acknowledge the real decision: renting equipment and a weekend of risky DIY vs. a done-for-you crew
Show a local before/after and a short homeowner quote ('My house looks brand new') to build trust
Reassure on safe, surface-appropriate techniques so they won't risk damaging siding, pavers, or roof
Make booking effortless — reply, call, or text to confirm a date; quote stays valid and no-obligation

campaigns.name: Booking Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Protect scheduled jobs by ensuring the property is accessible and ready, cutting last-minute cancellations and wasted truck rolls.
campaigns.trigger: Booking confirmed (24h-before reminder); cancellation/no-access event (recovery branch).
campaigns.segment: Any customer with a scheduled job; branch into no-access/cancellation recovery if the crew can't complete the visit.
campaigns.rationale: An in-home exterior job fails if cars block the driveway, gates are locked, or the customer cancels last-minute — a timed reminder protects revenue immediately, the same way appointment reminders cut no-shows by ~35%.

emails.purpose: Confirm tomorrow's visit, deliver the final prep steps, and offer a one-tap reschedule so a conflict becomes a new date instead of a no-show.
emails.subject: We'll see you tomorrow — a quick prep before we arrive
emails.preview: Move the cars, unlock the gate, bring in pets — and we'll handle the rest.
keyPoints: Confirm the service, date, and arrival window for tomorrow
Final prep checklist so the crew can start on time: clear vehicles, unlock side gates, secure pets, close windows
Note that someone doesn't need to be home for most exterior work, but how to reach the crew if questions come up
Friction-free reschedule/cancel link plus the direct phone/text line

campaigns.name: Seasonal Re-Booking & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Win back one-and-done customers with a seasonal refresh re-booking and upsell additional services (roof, pavers, sealing).
campaigns.trigger: Job completed + 6 months (spring/fall season change); branch by services already purchased.
campaigns.segment: Past customers ~6-12 months post-job; high-satisfaction customers get an expansion offer for services they haven't yet tried.
campaigns.rationale: FreshView's #1 churn risk is customers who simply forget to re-book the next season, and there's no seasonal outreach in place — a timed recap that reminds them how good their home looked recaptures repeat revenue and naturally upsells add-on services.

emails.purpose: Trigger a seasonal re-booking by reminding the customer of their last result, framing why now (pollen/mildew season), and offering an easy add-on.
emails.subject: Remember how good your home looked last time?
emails.preview: Georgia pollen and mildew creep back fast — let's keep that just-cleaned look.
keyPoints: Remind them of their last service and the transformation they loved
Frame the timing: spring pollen / fall mildew and algae build back up on siding, pavers, and roofs every season
Offer an easy add-on they haven't booked — e.g., add roof washing or paver sealing to this visit
One-tap re-book or reply to grab a spot before the busy season fills up

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed customers who haven't booked in 12+ months and clean the list of unresponsive contacts.
campaigns.trigger: 12+ months since last job with no re-booking or engagement.
campaigns.segment: Dormant past customers; final-email sunset branch for non-responders to protect deliverability.
campaigns.rationale: With no lapsed-customer reactivation flow, a year-plus of past customers are sitting dormant — a low-cost win-back recovers 5-15% of them, and FreshView's exterior surfaces visibly degrade over a year, giving an honest, concrete reason to return.

emails.purpose: Re-open the relationship with a warm 'we miss you,' remind them of the value, and lower the barrier with a fresh free quote.
emails.subject: It's been a while — your siding's probably ready
emails.preview: Come back and we'll make your home look brand-new again.
keyPoints: Warm, no-pressure 'it's been about a year since we cleaned your home'
Honest reminder that dirt, algae, and roof streaks build back up over a year in the Acworth/Marietta climate
Reassure them returning is easy — same trusted local crew, same satisfaction guarantee
Low-friction free no-obligation quote, with a small returning-customer incentive to tip the decision
